rule_name: API Activity from Unusual AWS Region
description: Detects API calls made in AWS regions outside the organization's expected operating regions. Attackers who steal credentials often operate from different geographic regions, and many organizations only use 1-3 AWS regions. Activity in unexpected regions may indicate credential theft and lateral movement.
query_kql: NOT awsRegion:"us-east-1" AND NOT awsRegion:"us-global" AND NOT eventName:"AssumeRole" AND NOT readOnly:"true"

threshold.rationale: A single off-region call may be a UI artifact. Three or more write operations in an unexpected region within 5 minutes indicates intentional activity.

triage_steps: Identify which region the activity occurred in
Determine if the organization has any legitimate workloads in that region
Check the user identity — is this a human user or service role?
Review the specific API calls — are they creating resources (EC2, Lambda)?
Check for crypto-mining indicators (RunInstances for GPU instance types in unusual regions)
false_positive_notes: Global services (IAM, STS, Route53) may log events with region 'us-east-1' regardless of where the call originates. AWS Console may pre-flight requests to multiple regions. Some services auto-replicate across regions.
tuning_guidance: Customize the expected_regions list for your organization. Start with a 7-day baseline to identify all regions your workloads use. Exclude read-only events to reduce noise. Focus on write/mutate operations in unexpected regions.
response_actions: Investigate the identity making off-region calls
Check for newly created resources in the unexpected region (EC2, Lambda, S3)
If unauthorized resources exist, terminate them immediately
Rotate credentials for the affected identity
Consider implementing SCP (Service Control Policy) to deny actions in unused regions
